content.problemStatement:
There are 10 types of viruses — one type with strength 0, one type with strength 1, and so on, up to the last type with strength 9.

Limak examines the behavior of a row of N viruses, indexed 1 through N. The initial situation is represented by a string **S** that consists of N digits, denoting the strengths of the viruses.

Stronger viruses will slowly defeat the weaker ones and convert them into their own type. While it's easy for a very strong virus to defeat a very weak one, the distance between them is an obstacle. Formally, we say that a virus with index x affects (tries to attack) a virus with index y (1 ≤ x, y ≤ N) if and only if:

strength(x) - strength(y) ≥ |x - y|

For example, a virus with strength 8 affects a virus with strength 5 if and only if the distance between them (the absolute difference between indices) is at most 3. Also, a virus always affects itself. Note that a virus can never affect stronger viruses.

In one second, simultaneously, each virus V is replaced with a virus whose type is the strongest among all the viruses that were affecting V at that moment.

Limak wants to know when this process will stop, i.e. when all N viruses will have the same type.
It can be proved that such a situation must happen eventually.
Can you help Limak and compute the number of seconds after which the process will stop?
constraints: Subtasks: Subtask #1 (20 points): 1 ≤ N ≤ 50, Subtask #2 (30 points): It's guaranteed that the answer won't exceed 10., Subtask #3 (10 points): S contains digits '0' and '1' only., Subtask #4 (40 points): Original constraints.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The only line of each test case contains one string S, consisting of digits '0' through '9'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing one integer — the number of seconds the process will last (after which all N viruses will have the same type).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: Test case 1. In the string 555755555, the virus with index 4 (the one with strength 7) affects two viruses to the left, and two viruses to the right, i.e. viruses with indices 2, 3, 5 and 6. These four viruses will be changed to strength 7 after the first second. The new string will be 577777555.

Explanation: The string changes in the following way: 123456789 initially, 223456789 after 1 second, 333456789 after 2 seconds, 333356789 after 3 seconds, 333366789 after 4 seconds, 333366689 after 5 seconds, 333366699 after 6 seconds, 333366699 after 7 seconds, 333366999 after 8 seconds.
Explanation: The string does not change because all viruses have the same strength.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we will simulate the process of viruses affecting each other. We will start with the initial string S and then iteratively update the string based on the rules of the problem. We will keep track of the number of seconds that have passed and stop the simulation when all viruses have the same type.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * seconds) — where seconds is the number of seconds the process will last. In the worst case, seconds can be up to N.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — we need to store the string S and the updated string at each step.
